goal: Fix RR-11: Redact known secret patterns from server.py log output and move full Claude Code stdout/stderr to DEBUG level. Include unit tests and documentation updates.

tasks.name: Add _redact_secrets helper to server.py
tasks.action: Add a `_redact_secrets(text: str) -> str` function near the top of server.py (after imports, before _expand_slash_command). It should use `re.sub` to replace values matching known secret env var patterns with `[REDACTED]`. Patterns to match: any value of CLAUDE_API_TOKEN, DYNAMIC_AGENT_KEY, MCP_API_TOKEN, PLAN_API_TOKEN, TESTER_API_TOKEN read from the environment at module load time. Build a single compiled regex from `re.escape(value)` for each non-empty token, joined with `|`. If no tokens are found, return text unchanged. Also add `import re` at the top.
tasks.verify: run_tests passes. Manually inspect that `_redact_secrets` is defined and uses `re.compile` with escaped token values.
tasks.done: `_redact_secrets` function exists in server.py, accepts a string, returns the string with all known token values replaced by `[REDACTED]`.

tasks.name: Move stdout/stderr logging to DEBUG and apply redaction
tasks.action: In both `/ask` and `/plan` endpoint handlers, change the four (or three) `logger.info(...)` lines that log `result.stdout`, `result.stderr`, `result.returncode`, and `result` to: (1) `logger.debug(f'stdout: {_redact_secrets(result.stdout)!r}')`, (2) `logger.debug(f'stderr: {_redact_secrets(result.stderr)!r}')`, (3) `logger.debug(f'returncode: {result.returncode}')`. Remove the `logger.info(f'result: {result!r}')` line entirely (it duplicates stdout+stderr+returncode and is hard to redact). Also wrap the `logger.error(f'Claude Code exited with error: {result.stderr}')` lines with `_redact_secrets`. Add a new `logger.info` line in each handler that logs only: `f'Claude Code completed: returncode={result.returncode} stdout_bytes={len(result.stdout)} stderr_bytes={len(result.stderr)}'` — a safe summary at INFO level.
tasks.verify: run_tests passes. `grep -n 'logger.info.*result.stdout' cluster/agent/claude/server.py` returns no matches. `grep -n 'logger.debug.*_redact_secrets' cluster/agent/claude/server.py` returns matches.
tasks.done: Full stdout/stderr logged at DEBUG level with redaction; INFO level logs only a safe summary (returncode + byte counts). No raw subprocess output at INFO.
blockers: Python tests (115/115) pass. Go tests fail with OS resource exhaustion: "failed to create new OS thread (have 3 already; errno=11)" and "may need to increase max user processes (ulimit -u)". This is a container-level infrastructure issue unrelated to the Python changes in server.py.

tasks.name: Make log level configurable via LOG_LEVEL env var
tasks.action: Read `os.environ.get('LOG_LEVEL', 'INFO')` and pass `getattr(logging, level_str.upper(), logging.INFO)` to `logging.basicConfig(level=...)`. Add `import os` at the top. This allows operators to set `LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG` in docker-compose to see full (redacted) output when debugging.
tasks.verify: run_tests passes. `LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG python -c 'import setuplogging; import logging; assert logging.getLogger().level == logging.DEBUG'` exits 0.
tasks.done: setuplogging.py reads LOG_LEVEL from environment, defaults to INFO.

tasks.name: Add unit tests for _redact_secrets and log-level changes
tasks.action: Add tests to test_server.py (which already mocks runenv): (1) `TestRedactSecrets` class: import `_redact_secrets` from server. Test that each dummy token value ('dummy-token', 'dummy-key', 'dummy-mcp-token', 'dummy-plan-token', 'dummy-tester-token') is replaced with '[REDACTED]' in arbitrary text. Test that text with no secrets is returned unchanged. Test that multiple occurrences in one string are all replaced. (2) `TestLogLevelOnSubprocessOutput` class: using `patch('server.subprocess.run')` and `patch('server.logger')`, call `/ask` with a valid token and assert that `logger.debug` was called (not `logger.info`) for stdout/stderr lines, and that `logger.info` was called with only the safe summary containing 'returncode='. Also add test.sh entry if test_server.py is not already listed (it is — confirmed).
tasks.verify: run_tests passes. `cd cluster/agent/claude && python -m pytest test_server.py -v --tb=short` shows all new tests PASSED.
tasks.done: At least 5 new test cases in test_server.py covering redaction correctness and DEBUG-level logging.

tasks.name: Update THREAT_MODEL.md to mark RR-11 resolved
tasks.action: In §6 Residual Risks, change `### RR-11: Claude Code Subprocess Stdout/Stderr Logged Fully` to `### ~~RR-11: Claude Code Subprocess Stdout/Stderr Logged Fully~~ — RESOLVED (2026-03-28)`. Replace the body with a status note: `- **Status:** Fixed. Full Claude Code stdout/stderr moved to DEBUG level with secret redaction. INFO level now logs only a safe summary (returncode + byte counts). A compiled regex built from all known token env vars replaces token values with [REDACTED] before any log emission. setuplogging.py supports LOG_LEVEL env var for operators who need DEBUG output. Regression tests in test_server.py verify redaction and log levels.` Keep Severity/Likelihood for historical reference. In §9 Recommended Mitigations table, change the RR-11 row Status from `Open` to `✅ Done (2026-03-28)` and strike through the Priority/Risk columns like the other resolved rows.
tasks.verify: grep 'RESOLVED.*2026-03-28' docs/THREAT_MODEL.md shows RR-11 line. grep 'RR-11.*Done' docs/THREAT_MODEL.md shows the mitigations table entry.
tasks.done: RR-11 is marked resolved in both §6 and §9 of THREAT_MODEL.md with accurate description of the fix.
